onboarding.title: Welcome to CivicGuide AI 🇮🇳
onboarding.subtitle: Your personal AI assistant for understanding elections
onboarding.feature1Desc: Tailored advice based on your age, state, and voter status.
onboarding.feature2Desc: Verify claims and understand the Model Code of Conduct instantly.
onboarding.feature3Desc: A clear roadmap from registration to seeing the final results.

header.autoDetectNote: Other languages auto-detected from your browser.

overview.heroSubtitle: Embark on a guided journey to master the election process in minutes.
overview.description: India conducts the largest democratic elections on the planet. With over 97 crore registered voters and nearly 10 lakh polling stations, every election is a massive civic enterprise.

registration.summary: Get your EPIC card and enrol in the Electoral Roll.

campaigning.summary: Rules for parties and candidates during elections.

polling.summary: The EVM process, indelible ink, and booth rights.

results.summary: How votes are counted and governments formed.

overview.helplinkDesc: Your digital polling companion for registration and booth tracking.
overview.dykText: Pune has over 82 lakh registered voters. The Collectorate coordinates election logistics across 21 segments.

registration.title: Voter Registration
registration.subtitle: Your First Step to Democracy
registration.description: Before you can cast your vote, you must be enrolled in the Electoral Roll.
registration.epicSubtitle: Your primary identity credential for elections
epicPoints: Now available as digital e-EPIC from voters.eci.gov.in
Accepted for banking and official KYC purposes

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.detail: 18+ on 1st January of the election year

registration.stepsTitle: How to Register — Step by Step

steps.title: Submit Documents
steps.detail: Provide proof of age and address

campaigning.title: Campaigning & Model Code of Conduct
campaigning.subtitle: Rules of Engagement
campaigning.description: Campaigning is how candidates communicate their vision. The MCC ensures elections remain free, fair, and peaceful.
campaigning.mccDesc: Guidelines issued by the Election Commission to ensure free and fair elections.

mccPoints.title: No Hate Speech
mccPoints.detail: No appeals based on religion or caste

mccPoints.title: No Bribery
mccPoints.detail: Distributing cash or gifts is a crime

mccPoints.title: Silence Period
mccPoints.detail: 48 hours before polling, all campaigning stops

campaigning.expenseWarning: Exceeding these limits is an electoral offence.
campaigning.dykText: The MCC comes into force the moment election dates are announced by the ECI.
campaigning.aiAskQuery: What are the Model Code of Conduct rules during election campaigning in India?

polling.title: Polling Day
polling.subtitle: Your Day to Vote
polling.description: Cast your vote using EVMs, verified by VVPAT paper trail.
polling.evmVvpatSubtitle: How India's tamper-proof voting system works.
evm: Press button next to your candidate

steps.title: Ink & Slip
steps.detail: Get ink mark and voter slip

polling.dykText: The indelible ink has been used in Indian elections since 1962.

results.title: Election Results
results.subtitle: The Verdict of the People
results.description: Counting of votes is conducted transparently under strict security.

governmentFormation.title: Coalition
governmentFormation.detail: Parties can join to reach majority

keyConcepts.meaning: First Past The Post — highest votes wins

results.dykText: The counting of votes for the entire country is usually completed in a single day.

quiz.title: Voter Eligibility Quiz
quiz.subtitle: Find out if you are eligible to vote in India.
quiz.disclaimer: For educational purposes only. Verify at voters.eci.gov.in
nextStepsList: If not registered, fill Form 6 online
Carry your Voter ID or any valid photo ID

questions_data.question: How old are you?
questions_data.subtext: You must be 18 years or older on January 1 of the year you register.

options.label: Under 18
options.feedback: You're on your way! You need to be at least 18 years old to vote. 🌟

options.label: 18–25 years
options.feedback: First-time voters are the heart of democracy! 🎉

options.label: 26–60 years
options.feedback: You're in the prime of your civic life! 💪

options.label: Over 60
options.feedback: Senior voters bring decades of wisdom. Your voice matters! 🌺

questions_data.question: Are you a citizen of India?
questions_data.subtext: Only Indian citizens are entitled to vote in Indian elections.

options.label: Yes, Indian citizen
options.feedback: Wonderful! As an Indian citizen, you are entitled to vote. 🇮🇳

options.label: No, foreign citizenship
options.feedback: Voting is reserved for Indian citizens. You can still engage with democracy through civic education!

options.label: OCI / PIO status
options.feedback: OCI / PIO cardholders are not eligible to vote. Indian nationality can be taken up formally.

questions_data.question: Have you been declared of unsound mind by a court?
questions_data.subtext: Section 16 of the Representation of the People Act, 1950.

options.label: No
options.feedback: Great! This criterion doesn't apply to you. Let's continue.

options.label: Yes
options.feedback: A legal declaration of unsound mind creates a temporary disqualification. If revoked, eligibility is restored.

questions_data.question: Have you been disqualified for corrupt electoral practices?
questions_data.subtext: E.g., convicted of election offences under the RPA 1951.

options.label: No
options.feedback: Excellent! You're clear on this criterion. Nearly there!

options.label: Yes, convicted
options.feedback: A conviction for election offences leads to a disqualification period. Once it lapses, you become eligible again.

questions_data.question: Are you enrolled in the Electoral Roll?
questions_data.subtext: Your name must appear in the Electoral Roll to cast a valid vote.

options.label: Yes, enrolled
options.feedback: You're all set! Verify at voters.eci.gov.in. See you at the polling booth!

options.label: Not yet, but I will
options.feedback: Visit voters.eci.gov.in or fill Form 6 to register. It takes just minutes!

options.label: I don't know how
options.feedback: Don't worry — visit voters.eci.gov.in and click 'Register as New Voter'. Your BLO can also help!

mythbuster.title: Election Myth Buster
mythbuster.subtitle: Check if an activity is allowed under MCC or if a claim is a myth.
mythbuster.inputLabel: Enter a claim or activity to verify:
mythbuster.placeholder: e.g., Is it allowed to distribute food at rallies?
mythbuster.pendingDesc: Enter a claim on the left to see the AI verification result.
mythbuster.footerNote: Always consult official ECI guidelines for legal compliance

welcome.short: Hi! I'm CivicGuide 🇮🇳 — your AI assistant for everything about Indian elections.
steps: How do I register to vote?
welcome.tip: Ask me anything about the election process!
